# Performance

This document is the authoritative reference for Kasane's performance characteristics: principles, benchmark data, bottleneck analysis, and optimization status.
For measurement and profiling workflows, see [profiling.md](./profiling.md).

**Last verified**: 2026-03-17 (tables auto-generated by `tools/update-measurements.sh`)

## Principles

Ordered by tier. Within each tier, original numbering is preserved for cross-references.

**Tier 1 — Inviolable** (violations are bugs):

8. Prefer exactness-preserving optimizations first; when exactness is intentionally weakened, document it in [semantics.md](./semantics.md).
7. Cache only where invalidation can be expressed clearly enough to preserve correctness. Treat caching as part of the rendering policy, not as an invisible implementation detail.

**Tier 2 — Priority** (trade-offs require ADR):

2. Reducing unnecessary invalidation matters more than making already-cheap pure computation marginally faster.
6. Measure before optimizing; performance work must be grounded in benchmarks and replayable workloads.

**Tier 3 — Guideline** (inform design, not hard constraints):

1. Terminal and backend I/O dominate end-to-end latency more than `view()` or `layout()`.
3. Allocation behavior on hot paths matters because jitter is more harmful than raw throughput regressions.
4. Plugin overhead must stay bounded and predictable in both native and WASM paths.
5. TUI and GUI backends share semantics, but have different dominant costs and different fast paths.

## Service-Level Objectives

<!-- BENCH:slo -->
| Metric | SLO | Current | Headroom |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full frame CPU (80×24) | p99 < 200 μs | 58.7 us | 3.4× |
| Per-plugin WASM overhead | < 3 μs/plugin | 1.8 us | 1.6× |
| Per-frame allocation (warm) | < 150 allocs | 100 | 1.5× |
| state.apply(Draw) 100 lines | < 200 μs | 131.5 us | 1.5× |
<!-- /BENCH:slo -->

## Degradation Model

```
frame_cost(w, h, n) ≈ base_cpu(w,h) + salsa_sync(w,h) + plugin_overhead(n) + backend_io(changed)
```

- `base_cpu(80,24)` ≈ 57 μs, sub-linear with area
- `salsa_sync` ≈ 0.2–7 μs (AppState sync is unconditional; plugin contribution sync is skipped when no plugin needs re-collection via `view_deps()`)
- `plugin_overhead` ≈ 1.8 μs × n (WASM CM)
- `backend_io` ≈ 44–228 μs (TUI, terminal I/O dominated)

## Frame Execution Flow

```
Event received
  |
  v
Event batch processing (try_recv drains all pending)
  |
  v
state.apply()           -- O(dirty lines * atoms) for Draw with warm CursorCache; O(lines * atoms) on first frame / resize / scroll
  |
  v
sync_inputs_from_state() -- Salsa incremental sync (unconditional for AppState; plugin contributions skipped when view_deps() disjoint from dirty)
  |
  v
view(&state, &registry) -- Element tree construction (Salsa incremental memoization)
  |
  v
place(&element, area)   -- Layout calculation (flexbox + grid + overlay)
  |
  v
grid.clear() + paint()  -- CellGrid rendering
  |
  v
backend.present(&mut grid, result) -- O(changed_cells) -- diff + escape gen + I/O (dominant cost)
```

## Per-Frame Cost (80x24 Terminal)

Measured with `cargo bench --bench rendering_pipeline`.

<!-- BENCH:per_frame_cost -->
| Phase | Complexity | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `view()` | O(nodes) | 5.3 us (0 plugins) / 10.5 us (10 plugins) | Element tree + plugin contributions + Salsa sync |
| `place()` | O(nodes) | 1.2 us |  |
| `grid.clear()` | O(w*h) | 4.5 us |  |
| `paint()` | O(w*h) | 27.6 us | paint-only = clear+paint − clear |
| **CPU pipeline total** |  | **58.7 us** |  |
| `backend.present()` | O(changed_cells) | **14.5-49.1 us** | Incremental diff + SGR + I/O |
<!-- /BENCH:per_frame_cost -->

Note: paint-only cost is derived from the `paint/80x24` benchmark (which includes `grid.clear()`) minus `grid_clear/80x24`. `backend.present()` includes stdout write (50–500 μs, not benchmarked by criterion).

**Dominant cost: terminal I/O (`backend.present()`).**

## Existing Optimizations

| Optimization | Target | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| CompactString | Cell.grapheme | Avoids heap allocation for short strings (<=24B inline) |
| bitflags Attributes | Face.attributes | `Vec<Attribute>` -> u16. Copy-type eliminates allocation |
| Differential rendering | TuiBackend.present() | Only changed cells sent to terminal. Minimizes I/O |
| Event batching | main.rs | `try_recv()` drains all pending events, then renders once |
| SIMD JSON | protocol.rs | High-speed JSON parsing via simd_json |
| BufferRef | Element tree | Buffer lines referenced, not cloned. Zero-copy in view() |
| dirty_rows | CellGrid | Row-level dirty tracking skips unchanged rows in present() |
| Salsa incremental | render pipeline | Incremental recomputation of view, layout, plugin contributions, and overlays |
| detect_cursors two-strategy | state.apply | Attribute heuristic (fast path) + face-matching fallback |
| CursorCache incremental | state.apply | Per-line cursor cache; only dirty lines re-scanned on cursor-move frames |
| Layout reuse | render pipeline | Base layout from `backfill_surface_report_areas` reused in TUI path, avoiding redundant `flex::place()` |

## Declarative Pipeline Overhead

Additional cost of the declarative pipeline (`view() -> layout() -> paint()`) compared to the former imperative pipeline (`render_frame()` writing directly to CellGrid).

The view() cost includes Salsa incremental sync, plugin registry preparation, session/workspace metadata, and a richer element tree. Despite the increase from the original estimate, view() is still < 10% of the full frame, and the Salsa integration provides significant warm-cache benefits (see [Salsa Pipeline Benchmarks](#salsa-pipeline-benchmarks)).

### Net Declarative Overhead

| Phase | Initial Estimate | Measured |
|---|---|---|
| Element construction (view) | ~1-4 us | 5.0-10.4 us |
| Layout calculation (place) | ~1 us | 1.2 us |
| Recursive traversal (paint overhead) | ~0.3 us | Included in paint |
| **Total** | **~4-7 us** | **~7 us** (0 plugins) / **~12 us** (10 plugins) |

**7-12 us out of the full pipeline = 12-21%. Relative to terminal I/O (100-700 us) = 1-12%.**
**No practical impact on end-to-end latency.**

## Micro Benchmarks (10)

<!-- BENCH:micro -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Target | Measured | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `element_construct/plugins_0` | view() tree construction (0 plugins) | < 10 us | 5.3 us | OK (1.8× headroom) |
| `element_construct/plugins_10` | view() tree construction (10 plugins) | < 15 us | 10.5 us | OK (1.4× headroom) |
| `flex_layout` | place() layout calculation | < 5 us | 1.2 us | OK (4.1× headroom) |
| `paint/80x24` | clear() + paint() combined | -- | 32.1 us |  paint-only: 27.6 us |
| `paint/200x60` | clear() + paint() combined (large) | -- | 115.3 us |  paint-only: 88.6 us |
| `paint/80x24_realistic` | clear() + paint() combined (varied Face/lengths) | -- | 36.5 us |  paint-only: 32.0 us |
| `grid_clear/80x24` | clear() standalone | -- | 4.5 us |  |
| `grid_clear/200x60` | clear() standalone (large) | -- | 26.7 us |  |
| `grid_diff/full_redraw` | diff() first frame | -- | 39.1 us |  First frame only |
| `grid_diff/incremental` | diff() no changes | -- | 12.1 us |  Steady-state path |
<!-- /BENCH:micro -->

## Integration Benchmarks (8)

<!-- BENCH:integration -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Target | Measured | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `full_frame` | view -> layout -> paint -> diff -> swap | < 16 ms | 58.7 us | OK (272.5× headroom) |
| `draw_message` | state.apply(Draw) + full frame | < 5 ms | 59.6 us | OK (83.8× headroom) |
| `menu_show/items/10` | Menu display + full frame | < 5 ms | 72.8 us | OK (68.6× headroom) |
| `menu_show/items/50` | Menu 50 items + full frame | < 5 ms | 73.0 us | OK (68.4× headroom) |
| `menu_show/items/100` | Menu 100 items + full frame | < 5 ms | 69.9 us | OK (71.5× headroom) |
| `incremental_edit/lines/1` | 1 line edit -> view + paint + diff | -- | 51.8 us |  |
| `incremental_edit/lines/5` | 5 line edit -> view + paint + diff | -- | 53.2 us |  |
| `message_sequence` | draw_status + draw -> full frame | -- | 60.4 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:integration -->

`menu_show` is independent of item count because `menu_max_height=10` caps the visible rows.

## Extended Benchmarks

### JSON-RPC Parsing

<!-- BENCH:parse_request -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `parse_request/draw_lines/10` | JSON-RPC parse (10-line draw) | 58.3 us |  |
| `parse_request/draw_lines/100` | JSON-RPC parse (100-line draw) | 509.3 us |  |
| `parse_request/draw_lines/500` | JSON-RPC parse (500-line draw) | 2.52 ms |  |
| `parse_request/draw_status` | JSON-RPC parse (draw_status) | 2.9 us | Small message, high frequency |
| `parse_request/menu_show_50` | JSON-RPC parse (menu_show 50 items) | 51.0 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:parse_request -->

### State Application

<!-- BENCH:state_apply -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `state_apply/draw_lines/23` | state.apply(Draw) standalone | 31.3 us | Includes detect_cursors + compute_lines_dirty |
| `state_apply/draw_lines/100` | state.apply(Draw) standalone | 131.5 us | O(lines × atoms) cursor scanning |
| `state_apply/draw_lines/500` | state.apply(Draw) standalone | 701.2 us |  |
| `state_apply/draw_status` | state.apply(DrawStatus) | 1.2 us |  |
| `state_apply/menu_show_50` | state.apply(MenuShow) | 4.8 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:state_apply -->

The `state_apply/draw_lines` numbers above reflect the cold-cache / all-lines-dirty path (first frame, resize, scroll, file open). On typical cursor-move frames, `detect_cursors_incremental` re-scans only the 2 dirty lines (~1.2 μs for 23-line viewport), reducing the Draw cost from ~31 μs to ~17 μs. The full-scan path remains unchanged and dominates when Kakoune sends a new viewport (24-80 lines); the worst practical case is still ~132 μs (100 lines).

### Scaling Characteristics

<!-- BENCH:scaling -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `scaling/full_frame/80x24` | Full frame at 80x24 | 55.6 us | Baseline |
| `scaling/full_frame/200x60` | Full frame at 200x60 | 224.4 us |  |
| `scaling/full_frame/300x80` | Full frame at 300x80 | 423.1 us |  |
| `scaling/parse_apply_draw/500` | Parse + apply (500 lines) | 3.19 ms |  |
| `scaling/parse_apply_draw/1000` | Parse + apply (1000 lines) | 6.40 ms | Near-linear |
| `scaling/diff_incremental/80x24` | diff() no-change 80x24 | 12.2 us |  |
| `scaling/diff_incremental/200x60` | diff() no-change 200x60 | 76.4 us |  |
| `scaling/diff_incremental/300x80` | diff() no-change 300x80 | 149.8 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:scaling -->

Full frame scales sub-linearly with area (cache effects). diff() scales linearly.

JSON bytes -> parse -> apply -> render -> backend.present -> escape bytes.

<!-- BENCH:e2e_pipeline -->
| Benchmark | What It Measures | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `e2e_pipeline/json_to_escape_80x24` | Full pipeline (uniform data) | 167.2 us |  |
| `e2e_pipeline/json_to_escape_realistic` | Full pipeline (realistic data) | 123.7 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:e2e_pipeline -->

## Allocation Breakdown (Per-Phase)

Measured with `--features bench-alloc` (debug profile, single frame at 80x24).

<!-- BENCH:alloc_breakdown -->
| Phase | Alloc Count | Bytes | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| view | 57 | 17344 | Element tree construction |
| place | 29 | 1052 | Layout result vectors |
| paint | 3 | 1196 | Atom-to-cell conversion |
| **full_frame total** | **100** | **292808** | |
| parse_request (100 lines) | 1851 | 276066 | JSON parsing dominates |
<!-- /BENCH:alloc_breakdown -->

**Key finding**: `TuiBackend::present()` performs incremental diff internally (zero allocation for the diff phase). JSON parsing allocates heavily but is amortized by simd_json's speed.

## Allocation Hotspots (Code Analysis)

| Location | What | Frequency |
|---|---|---|
| `paint.rs` `atoms.to_vec()` | Atom slice duplication | lines * frames |
| `paint.rs` `ch.to_string()` | Grapheme -> String conversion | cells * frames |
| `view/mod.rs` `atom.contents.clone()` | String clone in status bar construction | status_atoms * frames |

The paint/view allocations are relatively small (3-6 allocs).

## Latency Distribution

Measured over 10,000 iterations (release profile). Values from local measurement; not auto-generated.

| Phase | p50 | p90 | p99 | p99.9 | max |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Full frame** | 55.1 us | 56.7 us | 81.7 us | 90.0 us | 115.3 us |
| view() | 5.1 us | 5.1 us | 6.2 us | 10.8 us | 12.4 us |
| place() | 1.2 us | 1.2 us | 1.5 us | 5.2 us | 6.5 us |
| clear()+paint() | 31.5 us | 31.7 us | 37.1 us | 42.6 us | 49.1 us |
| diff() | 12.5 us | 12.6 us | 17.3 us | 20.2 us | 39.5 us |

Tail latency is well-controlled. The p99.9 full frame (90.0 us) is only 1.63x the p50 (55.1 us). The max (115.3 us) stays well under 1 ms.

### Latency Budget Tests

All pass (release profile):

| Test | Budget | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `state_apply_under_200us` | < 200 us | Pass |
| `parse_request_under_500us` | < 500 us | Pass |
| `full_frame_under_2ms` | < 2 ms | Pass |

## Salsa Pipeline Benchmarks

Measured with `cargo bench --bench rendering_pipeline -- salsa`.

### Salsa Input Synchronization

Overhead of syncing AppState changes into Salsa tracked inputs.

<!-- BENCH:salsa_sync -->
| Benchmark | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `salsa_sync_inputs/buffer_content/23_lines` | 2.2 us | Typical viewport |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/buffer_content/59_lines` | 5.1 us | Large viewport |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/buffer_content/79_lines` | 7.0 us |  |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/buffer_content_realistic_23` | 1.6 us | Realistic face distribution |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/buffer_cursor_only` | 244.0 ns | Cursor move, no buffer change |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/status` | 213.5 ns | Status bar update only |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/menu_100_items` | 3.7 us |  |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/all_flags_80x24` | 3.1 us | All flags dirty at 80x24 |
| `salsa_sync_inputs/all_flags_300x80` | 7.6 us | All flags dirty at 300x80 |
<!-- /BENCH:salsa_sync -->

Cursor-only updates and status updates are extremely cheap, enabling efficient incremental recomputation.

### Salsa vs Legacy Pipeline

<!-- BENCH:salsa_vs_legacy -->
| Scenario | Salsa | Legacy | Difference |
|---|---|---|---|
| Full cold (80x24) | 43.7 us | **43.0 us** | +1.7% |
| Menu select warm | **46.6 us** | 51.8 us | -10.1% |
| Incremental edit | 48.3 us | **46.9 us** | +2.9% |
<!-- /BENCH:salsa_vs_legacy -->

Salsa's value emerges in **warm-cache scenarios**: menu selection (the most interactive operation) benefits from Salsa skipping unchanged computations.

### Salsa Scaling Advantage

<!-- BENCH:salsa_scaling -->
| Screen size | Salsa | Legacy | Improvement |
|---|---|---|---|
| 80×24 | **53.0 us** | 55.6 us | -4.7% |
| 200×60 | **152.4 us** | 224.4 us | -32.1% |
| 300×80 | **256.9 us** | 423.1 us | -39.3% |
<!-- /BENCH:salsa_scaling -->

At larger screen sizes, Salsa's incremental computation provides significant speedup over the legacy pipeline by avoiding redundant recomputation of unchanged element subtrees and layout regions.

### Salsa Scene Path

<!-- BENCH:salsa_patched -->
| Benchmark | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `salsa_scene/cold` | 26.4 us | Scene cold start |
| `salsa_scene/warm` | 5.1 us | Scene warm (Salsa-cached DrawCommands) |
<!-- /BENCH:salsa_patched -->

<!-- BENCH:line_dirty -->
| Benchmark | Measured | Notes |

| `line_dirty_single_edit` | 13.9 us | 1 line changed: selective_clear + paint |
| `line_dirty_all_changed` | 11.5 us | All lines dirty: full repaint |
| `line_dirty_buffer_status/1_line_changed` | 15.5 us | BUFFER|STATUS combo, 1 line |
<!-- /BENCH:line_dirty -->

## Bottleneck Analysis

Ranked by severity with current measurements.

### Severity: Medium

#### `detect_cursors()` Cost in state.apply(Draw)

`detect_cursors_incremental()` maintains a per-line `CursorCache` so that only dirty lines are re-scanned. On cursor-move frames (2 dirty lines), the attribute scan drops from ~15.6 μs to ~1.2 μs. The cache falls back to a full scan when line count changes, `lines_dirty` is unavailable, or the face-matching fallback was used on the previous frame.

<!-- BENCH:bottleneck_detect_cursors -->
| Lines | state_apply(Draw) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `state_apply/draw_lines/23` | 31.3 us | Full-scan path (first frame / all dirty) |
| `state_apply/draw_lines/100` | 131.5 us | Full-scan path |
| `state_apply/draw_lines/500` | 701.2 us | Exceeds 200 μs budget |
| `detect_cursors/incremental_2_dirty` | 1.2 us | Warm cache, 2 dirty lines (cursor move) |
| `detect_cursors/incremental_all_dirty` | 14.6 us | Warm cache, all lines dirty |
<!-- /BENCH:bottleneck_detect_cursors -->

Kakoune sends only viewport lines (24-80), so the worst practical case is ~132 μs (100 lines), within the latency budget. Remaining optimization opportunity:
- Display width caching (per-atom `atom_display_width` still dominates the per-line scan cost)

For resolved bottlenecks (Buffer Line Cloning, Container Fill Loop, diff() Allocation Dominance, grid.diff() Exceeds Target), see [ADR-015 in decisions.md](./decisions.md#adr-015-rendering-pipeline-performance-improvements).

### Severity: Low

#### word_wrap Vec Allocation

`render_wrapped_line()` allocates 2 Vecs per call. Only triggered during info popup display (infrequent).

#### Large Element Trees Without Virtualization

1,000+ node trees could reach ~50 us for construction + layout. Acceptable now, but 10,000+ nodes would compete with terminal I/O.

**Future mitigation**: `Element::VirtualList` for visible-range-only rendering.

## Optimization Status

For historical context on the compiler-driven optimization (superseded by Salsa incremental computation), see [ADR-010 in decisions.md](./decisions.md#adr-010-compiler-driven-optimization--svelte-like-two-layer-rendering).

For rendering pipeline improvements (zero-alloc diff, draw_grid, line-dirty expansion, container fill optimization), see [ADR-015 in decisions.md](./decisions.md#adr-015-rendering-pipeline-performance-improvements).

## WASM Plugin Benchmarks

Measured with `cargo bench -p kasane-wasm-bench` (wasmtime 42, Component Model, criterion).
See [ADR-013](./decisions.md#adr-013-wasm-plugin-runtime--component-model-adoption) for the full decision record.

### Raw WASM Overhead

<!-- BENCH:wasm_raw -->
| Benchmark | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `empty_call/wasm_noop` | 26.0 ns | WASM boundary crossing cost |
| `integer_call/wasm_add` | 23.6 ns |  |
| `host_import/1x` | 29.0 ns | Guest → host function call |
| `host_import/10x` | 77.4 ns | ~5 ns per additional host call |
| `empty_call/native_noop` | 1.2 ns | Baseline comparison |
<!-- /BENCH:wasm_raw -->

### Component Model Call Overhead

<!-- BENCH:wasm_cm -->
| Function | Raw Module | Component Model | Ratio | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| noop | 26.0 ns | **551.2 ns** | 21.2x | ~525.2 ns overhead |
| add | 23.6 ns | **546.6 ns** | 23.2x | ~523.0 ns overhead |
| echo_string 100B | 57.1 ns | **749.7 ns** | 13.1x | ~692.6 ns overhead |
| build_gutter 24 | 1.5 us | **6.1 us** | 4.1x | ~4.6 μs canonical ABI overhead |
| on_state_changed | 41.6 ns | **766.8 ns** | 18.4x | ~725.2 ns overhead |
| contribute_lines 24 | 72.5 ns | **1025.1 ns** | 14.1x | ~952.6 ns overhead |
| full_cycle | 114.3 ns | **1799.3 ns** | 15.7x | ~1.7 μs canonical ABI overhead |
<!-- /BENCH:wasm_cm -->

### Realistic Plugin Simulation

<!-- BENCH:wasm_realistic -->
| Scenario | Measured | Budget (~57 μs) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 plugin full frame | **1.8 us** | **3%** |  |
| 3 plugins full frame | **5.3 us** | **9%** |  |
| 5 plugins full frame | **8.7 us** | **14.8%** |  |
| 10 plugins full frame | **17.5 us** | **29.7%** |  |
| Cache hit (no state change) | **0.3 ns** | **0%** | DirtyFlags skip |
<!-- /BENCH:wasm_realistic -->

Scaling is linear at **~1.8 μs per plugin**.

### WASM vs Native Comparison

<!-- BENCH:wasm_native -->
| Operation | Native | WASM (CM) | Ratio | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| cursor_line full cycle | 9.3 ns | 1799.3 ns | 193.5x | Absolute cost (2 μs) is negligible |
| gutter_24 | 1618.5 ns | 6074.0 ns | 3.8x | Ratio drops with real computation |
<!-- /BENCH:wasm_native -->

### Instantiation Cost (Startup Only)

<!-- BENCH:wasm_instantiation -->
| Operation | Measured | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `cm_instantiation/component_new` | 9.90 ms | One-time at startup |
| `instantiation_scaling/instantiate_n/1` | 28.8 us | Per-Store |
| `instantiation_scaling/instantiate_n/5` | 129.4 us |  |
| `instantiation_scaling/instantiate_n/10` | 268.9 us |  |
<!-- /BENCH:wasm_instantiation -->

Total startup for 10 plugins ≈ 10 ms. Cacheable via `Engine::precompile_component`.

## High Refresh Rate (240Hz)

The CPU pipeline uses <2% of the 4.17 ms budget at 80×24. 240fps is achievable for the GPU backend with animation path separation (animation frames bypass view/place/paint, only updating scroll offset + GPU draw). See [ADR-015 in decisions.md](./decisions.md#adr-015-rendering-pipeline-performance-improvements) for analysis.
